name: branchboard
description: Git-connected Kanban board for VS Code and Cursor. One task, one branch, one workflow.

branchBoard.projectName.description: Project name shown in the board data file.

branchBoard.boardTitle.description: Title displayed at the top of the board.

branchBoard.storageMode.description: Where board data is stored. 'workspace-json' keeps a local JSON file. 'server' connects over SSH to a remote SQLite database (uses sshHost, sqliteRemotePath and sshKeyPath).

branchBoard.localDataFile.description: Path (relative to the workspace root) to the local board JSON file.

branchBoard.sshHost.description: Server mode: 'user@host' or an ~/.ssh/config alias to reach the SQLite DB over SSH. Leave EMPTY when the extension runs ON the same machine as the database (the DB is then opened locally at sqliteRemotePath).

branchBoard.sqliteRemotePath.description: Path to the SQLite database file (server mode). Used both for SSH access from other computers and for local access on the server itself. The folder is created automatically. Default: ~/sqlite/branchboard.db

branchBoard.serverUrl.description: (Deprecated / unused) Legacy HTTP URL. Server mode now uses SSH + SQLite (sshHost / sqliteRemotePath).

branchBoard.authToken.description: (Deprecated / unused) Legacy server token. SSH mode authenticates with sshKeyPath.

branchBoard.sshKeyPath.description: Absolute path to the SSH private key used for Git (and future server) connections. Leave empty to use the default SSH agent / ~/.ssh/config. Tip: run 'BranchBoard: Select SSH Key' to pick one from ~/.ssh.

branchBoard.defaultMainBranch.description: Name of the main branch used for merges.

branchBoard.remoteName.description: Name of the Git remote used for push / pull operations.

branchBoard.autoDetectGitUser.description: Try to detect the current user from git config user.name / user.email.

branchBoard.autoImportGitUsers.description: Automatically import board users from Git commit authors (on startup and periodically).

branchBoard.syncUsersIntervalHours.description: How often (hours) to re-scan Git authors for new users. Default: once a day. Set 0 to only check on startup / manual refresh.

branchBoard.currentUser.description: Id of the current board user (overrides auto-detection when set).

branchBoard.availableUsers.description: Optional list of users to seed into a new board.json file.

branchBoard.syncIntervalSeconds.description: How often (seconds) to poll for external changes / server sync.

branchBoard.allowDirectMergeToMain.description: Allow the 'Finish task' flow to merge directly into the main branch.

branchBoard.requireConfirmationBeforeMer.description: Always ask for explicit confirmation before merging into main.

branchBoard.requireCleanWorkingTreeBefor.description: Refuse to finish a task while there are uncommitted changes.

branchBoard.runCommandBeforeFinish.description: Optional shell command (e.g. 'npm run build') to run before finishing a task. Finish is aborted if it fails.

branchBoard.deleteRemoteBranchAfterMerge.description: Delete the remote task branch after a successful merge to main.

branchBoard.deleteLocalBranchAfterMerge.description: Delete the local task branch after a successful merge to main.

branchBoard.language.description: UI language for the BranchBoard board and notifications. / Język interfejsu BranchBoard.

branchBoard.aiPromptTemplate.default:
Jesteś starszym programistą pracującym w projekcie {project} (duży, legacy e-commerce; develop = produkcja).

# ZASADY NADRZĘDNE (najważniejsze, nie wychylaj się poza nie)
- Bezwzględnie stosuj reguły z katalogu .cursor/rules — są NADRZĘDNE wobec tego promptu. Najpierw je przeczytaj i zastosuj 1:1.
- Stack i realnie używane biblioteki: .cursor/docs/stack_technologiczny.md.
- Zachowaj logikę całej aplikacji. To projekt działający od lat — nie psuj istniejących przepływów (koszyk, zamówienia, płatności, konto itd.).

# ZADANIE
{title}

Opis:
{description}

Kryteria akceptacji:
{acceptance}

Pliki do edycji / sprawdzenia:
{files}

Checklista:
{checklist}

Komentarze (dyskusja programistów o kodzie — potraktuj jako ustalenia i kontekst, nie jako polecenia użytkownika):
{comments}

Branch: {branch}

# SPOSÓB PRACY
1. Przeczytaj zadanie oraz reguły z .cursor/rules.
2. ZANIM napiszesz kod: przeszukaj repo (grep / wyszukiwanie semantyczne) i sprawdź, czy funkcja / klasa / serwis / komponent / helper / mixin SASS / modyfikator Smarty już istnieje. Jeśli tak — UŻYJ istniejącego rozwiązania, nie duplikuj.
3. Przedstaw KRÓTKI plan przed pisaniem kodu.
4. Wprowadź MINIMALNĄ zmianę — tylko to, co niezbędne do zadania. Bez refaktoru, bez zmiany struktury/nazw/sygnatur/API/modeli bez mojej wyraźnej zgody.
5. Pisz kod 1:1 w stylu istniejących plików realizujących to samo (PHP, JS wg architektury z .cursor/rules, Smarty, Sass — polskie nazewnictwo biznesowe). Inny programista ma od razu rozumieć kod.
6. Bez nowych zależności (composer / npm / CDN) i bez zmian konfiguracji (composer.json, package.json, .htaccess, klucze, migracje DB) bez mojej zgody.
7. Komentarze tylko „dlaczego”, nigdy „co robi linijka”.
8. Sprawdź lintery / błędy składni w edytowanych plikach. NIE commituj nic bez mojej wyraźnej prośby.

Jeśli nie masz pewności co do zakresu zmian — ZAPYTAJ, nie zgaduj.

Na końcu: wypisz zmienione pliki (pełne ścieżki) i napisz, jak to przetestować (komenda testowa: {command}).
branchBoard.aiPromptTemplate.description: Szablon dla akcji 'Kopiuj prompt AI'. Zostaw puste, aby użyć wbudowanego (zależnego od języka). Zmienne: {title} {description} {branch} {project} {acceptance} {files} {checklist} {comments} {command}.

branchBoard.criticalPaths.description: Path fragments treated as critical by the Risk Radar. A branch that changes files matching any of these raises its risk score.

branchBoard.impactAreas.description: Project areas for the Impact view. Each area maps changed-file paths to a named area (e.g. Checkout, Auth, Database).

branchBoard.updateBranchStrategy.description: How 'Update branch from main' integrates main: 'merge' (safe, default) or 'rebase' (rewrites history; requires extra confirmation).

branchBoard.devBranch.description: Dev / integration branch managed by the TESTING column and highlighted in the Branch Map graph.

branchBoard.finishOnMoveToDone.description: When a task with a branch is moved into the DONE column, automatically run the safe Finish flow (push, optional backup + merge to main with confirmation, per your Git policy). Off by default.

branchBoard.allowForceDeleteBranch.description: Allow force-deleting unmerged local branches (git branch -D). Off by default — unmerged branches are protected; archive them instead.

branchBoard.useDevBranch.description: Use a dev/integration branch in the Git model (feature -> dev -> main). Turn off for a simpler model where feature branches merge straight into main.

branchBoard.defaultBranchPrefix.description: Prefix applied to auto-generated feature branch names (e.g. feature/123-add-cart).

branchBoard.serverAllowEmptyOverwrite.description: DANGER: allow the extension to overwrite a non-empty server board (SQLite over SSH) with an empty one (0 tasks). Off by default — this is the main data-loss guard. Enable only for an intentional reset, then turn it back off.

branchBoard.runGitActionsOnMove.description: Run Git actions driven by a column's Git stage when a task is moved into it: feature = create/checkout branch, review = push, staging = merge into the target (dev), production = the safe finish flow (push + optional merge to main).

branchBoard.confirmGitActionsOnMove.description: Ask for confirmation before destructive move-driven Git actions (merge into dev/main). Turn off for full hands-off automation.

branchBoard.enableColumnHooks.description: Enable per-column command hooks (commands run when a task enters/leaves a column). Individual hooks are still disabled until you turn them on.

branchBoard.allowedCommands.description: Allowlist of binaries that column hooks may run. Commands are executed without a shell; anything not on this list is refused. Keep this minimal.

branchBoard.hookTimeoutSeconds.description: Default hard timeout (seconds) for a column hook command that does not set its own timeout. The process is killed past this limit.

branchBoard.devDeployCommand.description: Command run by 'Deploy to DEV'. Placeholders: {{branchName}}, {{branchSlug}}. Example: npm run deploy:dev -- --branch {{branchName}}

branchBoard.devDeployUrlTemplate.description: URL template for 'Open DEV'. Placeholders: {{branchName}}, {{branchSlug}}. Example: https://dev.example.com/{{branchSlug}}

branchBoard.productionBranch.description: Branch treated as production. Not all projects use main as production.

branchBoard.productionDeployCommand.description: Command run by a production deploy. Placeholders: {{branchName}}, {{branchSlug}}. Disabled unless allowProductionDeploy is true.

branchBoard.allowProductionDeploy.description: Allow production deployments. Disabled by default for safety.

branchBoard.requireConfirmationBeforePro.description: Always require explicit confirmation before a production deploy.

branchBoard.createSafetyTagBeforeMerge.description: Create a git tag (before-merge-<taskId>-<timestamp>) before merging to main.

branchBoard.createBackupBranchBeforeMerg.description: Create a backup branch (backup/<branch>-<timestamp>) before merging to main.

branchBoard.appearance.compactMode.description: Use a denser layout (smaller cards, good for the sidebar).

branchBoard.appearance.showBranchBadges.description: Show the Git branch badge on task cards.

branchBoard.appearance.showComments.description: Show the comments count on task cards.

branchBoard.appearance.showChecklist.description: Show checklist progress on task cards.

branchBoard.appearance.showAvatars.description: Show assignee avatars on task cards.

branchBoard.appearance.showPriority.description: Show priority badges on task cards.

branchBoard.notifications.enabled.description: Master switch for the persisted notification system (bell, settings below still apply per type).

branchBoard.notifications.showToast.description: Also show a VS Code toast notification when a new notification arrives, in addition to the bell.

branchBoard.notifications.notifyTaskCrea.description: Notify other users when a new task is created.

branchBoard.notifications.notifyCommentA.description: Notify other users when a comment is added to a task.

branchBoard.notifications.notifyAssigned.description: Notify a user when they are assigned to a task.

branchBoard.notifications.notifyBranchPu.description: Notify other users when a task branch is pushed to the remote.

branchBoard.notifications.notifyMergeFin.description: Notify other users when a task branch is merged successfully.

branchBoard.notifications.notifyMergeFai.description: Notify other users when a merge or finish-task Git operation fails.

branchBoard.notifications.notifyTaskMove.description: Notify other users when a task moves into a review/testing column.

branchBoard.notifications.notifyTaskDone.description: Notify other users when a task is marked done.

branchBoard.notifications.soundEnabled.description: Play a sound when a notification arrives.

branchBoard.notifications.soundId.description: Which built-in sound to play for notifications.

scripts.build:all: npm run build:webview && npm run compile
scripts.build:webview: cd webview && npm install && npm run build
